paragraph: Your gait metrics have been holding remarkably steady over the past week — speed, step length, and double support all clustering right where they've been, which is exactly what you want to see. The one genuine shift is walking asymmetry, which dropped by more than half today compared to the weekly average; that kind of pattern usually means better bilateral control, and it's worth noticing what felt different about today's conditions or timing. Steps are up 38% this week, driven by those solid days on the 24th, 28th, and 30th, and your body seems to be handling the volume without drift in the gait fundamentals. Sleep has been steady too, averaging just under eight hours with last night matching that rhythm.
